parameters: <center> After all of that, after everything that burned
We saw you walking through the village.
We followed. I hope that's alright.
You don't have to say it.
I walked past this place every day for years.
And I told myself someone else would do
You started out of your own free will.
And I stood at my window watching you clean
I thought: Who does he think he is?
None of this is your fault.
And I'm sorry it took all of this for us to
My mum cried this morning too.
She cried and then she got up and started
She said that's what you do.
You told me you were going to try.
I watched you every day. Your work is actually
really a lot, way beyond my grasp to be fair.
You know what my grandfather used to say?
That the forest holds onto things.
That the forest holds onto things.
He was talking about more than plants.
The knowledge. The memory. The people who care
And now, we hold onto it.
All of us. Together this time.
Golden coat, white beneath the throat to belly. Moves
deep into old canopy. Never approaches. Watched me for
ten minutes before vanishing. I don't think it's gone...
